value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents the `/rewind` (or `/undo`) command under "Rewind history with `/rewind`", describing it as a way to roll back a conversation thread to a previous stable checkpoint after an agent has made successive unwanted changes — without discarding the session. This directly matches the Checkpoint/rewind capability and is distinct from adjacent capabilities (no audit logging, code review, cost tracking, evals, or resume/fork conflation). The cell label `/rewind` is the exact command name used on the page, and the description accurately captures the feature: rolling back session history after unwanted multi-step agent changes during an active TUI session to course-correct without restarting.
